VAL-PERF-006.evidence: Code review confirms incremental rendering is fully implemented. The render_content_surface function (compositor/main.rs ~line 340) accepts a force_full boolean parameter. When force_full is false, it enters an incremental code path that: (1) computes which visual lines changed by comparing PREV_CURSOR_POS, PREV_DOC_LEN against current state, (2) determines the minimal line range [first_changed..last_changed] using byte_to_visual_line(), (3) clears only the affected lines via fill_rect() on the specific Y region, (4) calls draw_tt_sel_scroll_lines() which renders only lines within [first_vis_line..last_vis_line]. Full clear only occurs on first render, context switch, scroll change, or selection change. Serial log verification confirms: typing 7 characters produced dirty rects of 1280x20 (one text line height) + 1280x28 (status bar) + 1280x8 (shadow) — NOT full-screen 1280x800 transfers.
VAL-PERF-006.details:
Code-level evidence:

1. DIRTY LINE TRACKING: Static globals PREV_CURSOR_POS, PREV_DOC_LEN, PREV_SEL_START, PREV_SEL_END (lines ~120-128) track previous frame state. Updated at end of each render_content_surface call (line ~435).

2. INCREMENTAL PATH (render_content_surface, line ~370): When force_full=false, computes new_cursor_line and prev_cursor_line via layout.byte_to_visual_line(). first_changed = min(prev_cursor_line, new_cursor_line). If total line count unchanged and cursor stays on same line, last_changed = new_cursor_line (single line). Otherwise extends to max of old/new total lines.

3. PARTIAL CLEAR (line ~415): surf.fill_rect(0, clear_y, content_w, clamped_h, BG_CONTENT) clears only the vertical band covering [first_vis..last_vis] lines — NOT surf.clear() on entire surface.

4. PARTIAL DRAW (line ~425): layout.draw_tt_sel_scroll_lines() renders only characters on visual lines [first_vis_line..last_vis_line], skipping all other lines via the condition 'if vis >= first_vis_line && vis <= last_vis_line' (drawing/lib.rs ~line 1139).

5. FORCE_FULL CONDITIONS (event loop, line ~1609): force_full is true only for: first_render, context_switched, scroll offset changed, or selection changed. Normal single-character typing with no scroll/selection change goes through incremental path.

6. SERIAL LOG CONFIRMATION: After typing 'h','e','l','l','o',' ','w', serial shows 'present rects=3 1280x20 1280x28 1280x8' for keystroke frames. 1280x20 = exactly one line_height (20px) of content — confirming only the cursor line is transferred, not the full 800px content surface.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-PERF-006 passes. Incremental content rendering is fully implemented with dirty line tracking, partial surface clearing, and line-range-limited text rendering. Serial log confirms single-line (20px) dirty rects per keystroke instead of full-screen (800px) transfers.
